_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, scorecard-refine-local cron 2026-06-05 0050)
_meta.note: LOCAL batch (5): DC Council Ward 6 Charles Allen + At-Large Christina Henderson + At-Large Doni Crawford (appointed Jan 20 2026) + Ward 4 Janeese Lewis George (now mayoral cand) + Ward 3 Matthew Frumin. Evidence-based finalization w/ cited URLs. RUBRIC INTERPRETATION: refuse_state_overreach NULL for all 5 (DC has no state per Art. I §8 cl. 17). HIGH-BAR public_justice q0 applied: Henderson earns TRUE via affirmative 'I will not defund MPD, should keep its current size' 2020 statement; Allen + Lewis George earn FALSE (DC Police Union attacked Allen, Lewis George ran on 'absolutely divest from MPD' Oct 2019); Crawford FALSE (co-authored DCFPI 'Divesting from the Police Can Strengthen the District' June 2020); Frumin null (no clean MPD-backing statement clears HIGH BAR). HEADLINE CATCHES: (1) Allen = HARDEST-FALSE DC archetype — INTRODUCING SPONSOR of D.C. Law 24-242 noncitizen voting (election q4 HARD-FALSE), AUTHOR of RCCA (public_justice q1 HARD-FALSE, vetoed by Biden after Senate 81-14), FLOOR-AMENDMENT-OFFERER on Sanctuary Values Act that BLOCKED DOC->ICE transfers, AUTHOR of DC's red-flag + ghost-gun laws (US House testimony bio cites these as signature achievements). (2) Henderson = ONLY-TRUE-CELL DC INDEPENDENT — affirmative pro-MPD-staffing 2020 candidate + Secure DC YES Mar 5 2024 + Gaza ceasefire supporter; but FALSE on tax (FY22 millionaire tax YES), noncitizen voting YES, ghost-gun YES, RCCA override YES, AND HARD-FALSE on industry_capture q0 via her Committee on Health stewardship of D.C. Law 24-312 expanded minor (11+) vaccine consent without parental notification. (3) Crawford = DCFPI-PIPELINE PROGRESSIVE appointed Jan 20 2026 to fill Kenyan McDuffie At-Large seat; special election June 16 2026 vs Patterson/Silverman; only ~5 months on Council → mostly nulls, but co-authored DCFPI 'Divesting from the Police' (HARD-FALSE public_justice q0) and built $200M excluded-workers undocumented-cash-relief campaign (HARD-FALSE border q1/q2). (4) Lewis George = NATIONAL DEFUND-MPD ORIGIN STORY — Oct 2019 tweet 'I will absolutely divest from MPD'; 2020 primary won 55-43 on defund platform (The Intercept profile); CO-AUTHOR of FY22 millionaire tax w/ Allen + Nadeau (econ q2 HARD-FALSE); but TRUE on foreign_policy_restraint q0 as FIRST DC Council member to call for Gaza ceasefire Oct 12 2023; CORRECTION VS PROMPT HYPOTHESIS: Lewis George voted YES on Secure DC both readings (Trayon White alone Present) — q4 TRUE despite progressive profile (Bonds/Henderson/Pinto cluster pattern). Now running 2026 mayoral campaign. (5) Frumin = JEWISH WARD-3 EDUCATION-ADVOCATE D — christian_liberty q0 honestly NULL (Frumin is publicly Jewish, JUFJ-affiliated; not professing Christ is accurate, not a negative); pro-DCPS, anti-charter-parity ($40-50M/yr 'structural underfunding' framing) so family q0 FALSE; RCCA override YES quoted 'modernizes our criminal code' + Secure DC YES — split public_justice pattern (q1 FALSE, q4 TRUE).

profile.religion: Not publicly disclosed (no public profession of Christ or any faith located in bio, campaign site, or press coverage)
profile.background: DC Council Ward 6 since Jan 2 2015 (succeeded Tommy Wells, his former boss). Capitol Hill, wife Jordi Hutchinson, two children. CHAIR Judiciary & Public Safety Committee 2017-Jan 2025 (three council periods) — stripped of chair in CP26 under recall pressure, reassigned to Chair Transportation & Environment. Survived 2024 recall (Aug 12 2024, 5,500 sigs ~900 short). Term to Jan 2027; announced 2026 reelection (ended mayoral speculation).

charles-allen.notes_append: [2026-06-05 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified active (dccouncil.gov/council/councilmember-allen). HARDEST-FALSE DC PROFILE — multiple signature-bill catches: (election_integrity q4 HARD-FALSE) INTRODUCING SPONSOR of D.C. Law 24-242 Local Resident Voting Rights Amendment Act 2022 enabling noncitizens (including green-card holders AND undocumented residents) to vote in DC local elections — floor quote Oct 6 2022: 'This bill is in line with our D.C. values and this council's history of expanding the right to vote'; Congress has attempted repeal HR 192/HR 884. (public_justice q1 HARD-FALSE) AUTHOR/floor-manager of Revised Criminal Code Act 2022 lowering carjacking/robbery/burglary max sentences + expanding jury trials; Senate disapproved 81-14, Biden signed disapproval March 8 2023 — virtually unprecedented federal override of DC law and direct trigger of the 2024 recall. (border_immigration q1/q2 HARD-FALSE) Voted YES Dec 15 2020 unanimous final passage Sanctuary Values Act D.C. Law 23-282 AND offered the 13-0 Allen Amendment BLOCKING DC DOC from directly transferring released detainees to ICE custody (made bill MORE sanctuary, not less). (sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE) Co-sponsored B24-0808 Human Rights Sanctuary Amendment Act 2022 AND B24-0726 Enhanced Reproductive Health Protections 2022 — both DC abortion-sanctuary acts shielding out-of-state abortion AND gender-affirming-care seekers from investigation. (biblical_marriage q2 FALSE) Human Rights Sanctuary Act extends sanctuary protections to gender-affirmation care for minors. (self_defense q0/q1 HARD-FALSE) US House testimony bio (March 29 2023) lists 'creating the District's red flag gun safety law' and 'banning ghost guns' as signature achievements; authored two emergency firearms bills March 2020 + Ghost Guns Prohibition Emergency Amendment Act 2020 floor mover. (economic_stewardship q2 FALSE) Proposed his own $250K+ personal-income-tax increase FY21; voted YES on FY22 millionaire-tax budget July 20 2021. (family_child_sovereignty q0 FALSE) Introduced Public School Transparency Act 2019 imposing FOIA + open-meetings + donor-disclosure mandates on DC charters — Washington Examiner + FOCUS coded this as anti-charter constraint. (public_justice q0 HIGH-BAR FALSE) DC Police Union April 2022 ad attacked Allen as anti-cop 'experiment is a failure, and people are dying'; sponsored MPD discipline-out-of-CBA law police union opposes — HIGH BAR for pro-LE advocacy NOT met. (public_justice q3 FALSE) entire career frame is public-health/root-causes (civilianizing non-emergency MPD calls). (public_justice q4 FALSE) Voted YES on Secure DC B25-0345 both readings (Feb 6 + Mar 5 2024) — but offered McDuffie DNA-backlog softening carve-out under recall pressure; vote-yes-but-actively-weaken pattern → q4 FALSE per 'bear the sword' direction. (industry_capture q0 FALSE) Voted YES on DC COVID-19 student vaccine mandate (16-17yo first cohort); publicly stated city should mandate vaccines for educators + critical government functions. NULL: christian_liberty all 4 (no Christian profession); life q0/q2/q3/q4; marriage q0/q4; family q1/q2/q3; election q0/q1; refuse_state_overreach all 5 (DC has no state per Art. I §8 cl. 17); foreign_policy_restraint (met w/ JCRC during 2024 anti-ceasefire advocacy day — no sponsored cease-fire resolution; null per cite-only at local tier).

1.note: Co-sponsored B24-0808 Human Rights Sanctuary Amendment Act 2022 and B24-0726 Enhanced Reproductive Health Protections 2022 — both DC abortion-sanctuary acts shielding out-of-state abortion seekers.

2.note: Human Rights Sanctuary Amendment Act 2022 (co-sponsored) extends sanctuary protections to gender-affirmation care — shields out-of-state gender-affirming-care seekers including minors.

0.note: Introduced Public School Transparency Act 2019 imposing FOIA + open-meetings + donor-disclosure + board-composition mandates on DC charters — viewed by school-choice groups (FOCUS, Washington Examiner) as constraint, not expansion.

2.note: Proposed his own $250K+ personal-income-tax increase in FY21 budget; voted YES on FY22 millionaire-tax package July 20 2021.

4.note: HEADLINE: Allen was INTRODUCING SPONSOR of D.C. Law 24-242 Local Resident Voting Rights Amendment Act 2022 allowing noncitizens to vote in DC local elections; Oct 6 2022 floor quote: 'This bill is in line with our D.C. values and this council's history of expanding the right to vote.' Congress has attempted repeal (HR 192/HR 884).

0.note: HIGH-BAR NOT MET. DC Police Union April 2022 ad attacked Allen as anti-cop ('experiment is a failure, and people are dying'); Allen sponsored MPD discipline-out-of-CBA law police union opposes.

1.note: HEADLINE: AUTHOR/floor-manager of Revised Criminal Code Act 2022 lowering carjacking/robbery/burglary max sentences + expanding jury trials. Senate disapproved 81-14; Biden signed disapproval March 8 2023 — virtually unprecedented federal override of DC law. Direct trigger of 2024 recall.

3.note: Entire career frame = public-health/root-causes approach to crime: civilianizing non-emergency MPD calls, expanding violence-interruption funding.

4.note: Voted YES on Secure DC B25-0345 both readings (Feb 6 + Mar 5 2024) but offered DNA-backlog softening carve-out (McDuffie amdt) under recall pressure — vote-yes-but-actively-weaken; not 'bear the sword' magistrate posture.

1.note: Voted YES Dec 15 2020 final passage Sanctuary Values Amendment Act D.C. Law 23-282 (13-0 unanimous); offered the floor Allen Amendment (13-0) BLOCKING DC DOC from directly transferring released detainees to ICE custody — made bill MORE sanctuary.

2.note: Sanctuary Values Act floor amendment offerer.

0.note: Authored two emergency firearms bills March 2020 strengthening DC's already-strictest-in-nation gun laws.

1.note: HEADLINE: Allen's official US House testimony bio (March 29 2023) lists 'creating the District's red flag gun safety law' and 'banning ghost guns' as signature achievements; floor mover on Ghost Guns Prohibition Emergency Amendment Act 2020.

0.note: Voted YES on DC COVID-19 student vaccine mandate (16-17yo first cohort); publicly stated city should mandate vaccines for educators + critical government functions.

profile.religion: Not publicly disclosed (Wikipedia infobox blank; sorority is AKA, not religious)
profile.background: DC Council At-Large since Jan 2 2021. Born Oct 10 1985, Brooklyn NY; spouse Nu Wexler; one child; Petworth resident. Registered Independent (was Democrat until 2020 to claim one of DC's two charter-reserved non-majority At-Large seats). Former Schumer education-policy LA and DCPS staffer. Won 2020 special on moderate-on-policing posture; re-elected Nov 5 2024 with 24.3% — term to Jan 2029. CHAIR Committee on Health (key for vaccine + reproductive policy).

christina-henderson.notes_append: [2026-06-05 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified active (dccouncil.gov/council/at-large-councilmember-christina-henderson). RARE DC-INDEPENDENT cross-party catches on policing + Article-I restraint set against the typical progressive-D pattern: (public_justice q0 HARD-TRUE — HIGH-BAR MET) Wikipedia: 'skeptic of defunding the police, saying that the Metropolitan Police Department should keep its current size' — the cleanest DC pro-MPD-staffing 2020 candidate posture; Washington Business Journal labeled her the moderate-on-policing centrist. (public_justice q4 HARD-TRUE) Voted YES on Secure DC Omnibus B25-0345 final passage Mar 5 2024 (12 YES, Trayon White Present); restored pretrial detention, drug-free zones, expanded police authority. (foreign_policy_restraint q0 SOFT-TRUE) Washington City Paper named her among 'most consistent supporters of a Gaza ceasefire resolution' on DC Council — Article-I restraint catch. BUT FALSE on the typical DC-D suite: (economic_stewardship q2 HARD-FALSE) Voted YES on FY22 BSA July 20 2021 raising marginal income tax on filers >$250K; appeared on WAMU promoting the millionaire tax. (election_integrity q4 HARD-FALSE) Voted YES on D.C. Law 24-242 Local Resident Voting Rights Amendment Act Oct 18 2022 (12-0 with 1 abstention) allowing green-card AND undocumented residents to vote in DC local elections. (self_defense q1 FALSE) Voted YES Dec 6 2022 on Omnibus Firearm and Ghost Gun Clarification Amendment Act D.C. Law 24-347 tightening DC's firearms code. (public_justice q1 FALSE) Voted YES on RCCA veto override Jan 17 2023 (12-1, Trayon White lone NO). (family_child_sovereignty q1 HARD-FALSE + industry_capture q0 HARD-FALSE) As Committee on Health CHAIR, Henderson stewarded D.C. Law 24-312 Consent for Vaccinations of Minors Amendment Act 2022 (introduced by Gray + Cheh, championed via her committee) — allows minors as young as 11 to consent to ACIP-recommended vaccines WITHOUT parental notification and BARS providers/schools/insurers from notifying parents. (biblical_marriage q2/q4 FALSE) Washington Blade names Henderson among 'longtime LGBTQ rights supporters' and Capital Stonewall Democrats endorsed her; backed LGBT Budget Coalition's healthcare/HIV goals. NULL: christian_liberty all 4 (no Christian profession); life all (sworn in AFTER D.C. Law 23-90 + no direct vote located on D.C. Law 24-254 amendments; no PP-PAC funding cite); marriage q0/q3; family q0/q2/q3; election q0/q1; refuse_state_overreach all 5 (DC has no state); self_defense q0/q2/q3/q4.

2.note: Washington Blade names Henderson among 'longtime LGBTQ rights supporters'; backed LGBT Budget Coalition's healthcare/HIV goals; Capital Stonewall Democrats endorsement.

4.note: Capital Stonewall Democrats endorsed her; consistent pro-LGBTQ Pride / programming pattern.

1.note: HEADLINE: As Committee on Health CHAIR, Henderson stewarded D.C. Law 24-312 Consent for Vaccinations of Minors Amendment Act 2022 (introduced by Gray+Cheh) — minors as young as 11 may consent to ACIP-recommended vaccines WITHOUT parental notification; providers/schools/insurers BARRED from notifying parents.

2.note: Voted YES on FY22 BSA July 20 2021 raising marginal income tax on filers >$250K; appeared on WAMU promoting the millionaire tax.

4.note: Voted YES on D.C. Law 24-242 Local Resident Voting Rights Amendment Act Oct 18 2022 (12-0/1 abstention) allowing green-card AND undocumented residents to vote in DC local elections.

0.note: HEADLINE / HIGH-BAR MET — Wikipedia documents she was 'skeptic of defunding the police, saying that the Metropolitan Police Department should keep its current size.' The cleanest pro-MPD-staffing 2020 DC candidate; Washington Business Journal moderate-on-policing label.

1.note: Voted YES on RCCA veto override Jan 17 2023 (12-1, Trayon White lone NO) — RCCA reduced penalties on carjacking/violent crime.

4.note: Voted YES on Secure DC Omnibus Amendment Act of 2024 (B25-0345) final passage Mar 5 2024 (12 YES, Trayon White Present); restored pretrial detention, drug-free zones, expanded police authority.

1.note: Voted YES on Omnibus Firearm and Ghost Gun Clarification Amendment Act of 2022 (D.C. Law 24-347) first reading Dec 6 2022 (11-0).

0.note: Washington City Paper named Henderson among 'most consistent supporters of a Gaza ceasefire resolution' on DC Council — Article-I-restraint posture.
Committee on Health CHAIR stewardship of D.C. Law 24-312 minor-vaccine-consent expansion (11+ unilateral consent w/o parental notification) — HARD-FALSE on informed-consent direction.

profile.religion: Not publicly disclosed (no church affiliation in Wikipedia, dccouncil.gov bio, or campaign site)
profile.background: APPOINTED DC Council At-Large Jan 20 2026 by unanimous Council vote to fill Kenyan McDuffie seat after McDuffie resigned to run for mayor. Faces June 16 2026 special election vs Elissa Silverman + Cary Patterson. Independent (switched D->I in 2025 for At-Large seat eligibility under DC's charter-cap). BA Political Science/Spanish + MPIA, University of Pittsburgh. Career: Neighborhood Allies (Pittsburgh nonprofit) → DC Fiscal Policy Institute Policy Analyst (Jan 2019 - Mar 2022) → McDuffie's office (Senior Policy Advisor / Legislative Director / Committee Director Business & Economic Development, Mar 2022 - Jan 2026). Now serves on Judiciary & Public Safety Committee.

doni-crawford.notes_append: [2026-06-05 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified active appointed incumbent (dccouncil.gov/council/at-large-councilmember-doni-crawford). DCFPI-PIPELINE PROGRESSIVE just 5 months on Council → mostly nulls but the PAPER-TRAIL CATCHES from DCFPI work are signature: (public_justice q0 HEADLINE HARD-FALSE) Co-authored June 2020 DCFPI publication 'How Divesting from the Police Can Strengthen the District' explicitly urging DC Council to REJECT Mayor Bowser's proposed $17.5M MPD budget INCREASE and restore $4.6M cut to violence-interruption services — defund-adjacent paper-trail with her byline. (border_immigration q1/q2 HARD-FALSE) Authored DCFPI campaign for $200M cash assistance for 'excluded workers' explicitly including undocumented immigrants; helped secure $41M in DC budget for that population. (biblical_marriage q4 FALSE) Received inaugural Wanda Alston Legacy Award April 2026 (LGBTQ homeless youth foundation named for slain DC LGBT-rights director); foundation cited 'LGBTQ+ funding priorities' as her record. (economic_stewardship q2 FALSE) WJLA candidate forum: 'Crawford backed the concept of new taxes' (business activity tax + tax-abatement clawbacks); DCFPI mansion-tax / $74M wealthy-households-surcharge advocacy. (public_justice q1 FALSE) Testified IN SUPPORT of RESTORE Act 2021 (record-expungement simplification, DC Justice Lab bill); 51st interview called curfew zones 'short-term tools that aren't effective in the long-run' — soft-on-juvenile-crime signal. (public_justice q3 FALSE) Co-authored 'Divesting from the Police' framing violence as solvable by community-based services / violence interruption / housing — public-health-not-punishment framework. NULL: christian_liberty all 4 (no public profession); life all; marriage q2/q3; family all (out of tier on her short record); election all (DC's existing noncitizen-voting regime predates her but no direct vote/statement); refuse_state_overreach all 5 (DC has no state); self_defense all (DCFPI doesn't work this issue); industry_capture all; foreign_policy_restraint (not at LOCAL tier); border q3/q4 (E-Verify, foreign farmland N/A in DC).

4.note: Received inaugural Wanda Alston Legacy Award April 2026 (LGBTQ homeless youth foundation); foundation cited 'LGBTQ+ funding priorities' as her record.

2.note: WJLA candidate forum: 'Crawford backed the concept of new taxes'; DCFPI mansion-tax / $74M wealthy-households-surcharge advocacy under her byline.

0.note: HEADLINE HARD-FALSE: Co-authored June 2020 DCFPI 'How Divesting from the Police Can Strengthen the District' explicitly urging DC Council to REJECT $17.5M MPD budget INCREASE and restore $4.6M cut to violence-interruption — defund-adjacent paper-trail with her byline.

1.note: Testified IN SUPPORT of RESTORE Act 2021 (DC Justice Lab record-expungement); 51st interview called curfew zones 'short-term tools that aren't effective in the long-run.'

3.note: Co-authored paper frames violence as solvable by 'community-based services' / 'violence interruption' / housing — public-health-not-punishment framework.

1.note: Authored DCFPI campaign for $200M cash assistance for 'excluded workers' explicitly including undocumented immigrants; helped secure $41M for that population.

2.note: Excluded-workers framework actively built cash-benefit infrastructure for undocumented residents — opposite of preempting sanctuary.

profile.religion: Not publicly disclosed (Wikipedia infobox blank; DSA-affiliated)
profile.background: DC Council Ward 4 since Jan 2 2021 (ousted incumbent Brandon Todd 55-43 in 2020 D primary on DSA-backed defund-MPD platform). Born April 30 1988, Washington DC; Howard University; former DC OAG attorney. Re-elected 2024 (~66% primary). Now announced 2026 mayoral run (June 16 2026 primary). The most prominent DC progressive on Council; consistent anti-Zionist voice (first DC Council to call for Gaza ceasefire Oct 12 2023); openly embraced indicted Ward 8 CM Trayon White as 'mentor' May 2026.

janeese-lewis-george.notes_append: [2026-06-05 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified active incumbent running for mayor (dccouncil.gov/council/ward-4-councilmember-janeese-lewis-george). NATIONAL DEFUND-MPD ORIGIN STORY + ARTICLE-I-RESTRAINT CROSS-CATCH: (public_justice q0 HEADLINE HARD-FALSE) Oct 2019 tweet 'I will absolutely divest from MPD and put that money into violence interruption programs'; 2020 primary won 55-43 on defund platform; The Intercept profiled her as the national 'defund' test case; 2026 mayoral run has softened rhetoric but original record stands. (economic_stewardship q2 HARD-FALSE) CO-AUTHOR (with Allen + Nadeau) of July 2021 FY22 budget amendment raising marginal income tax on individuals over $250K to fund childcare, basic-income pilot, homelessness vouchers — the 'transformational' budget adopted July 20 2021. (public_justice q1 FALSE) Voted YES on RCCA veto override Jan 17 2023 (12-1, Pinto lone NO) AND publicly defended RCCA on her Ward 4 constituent dispatch. (public_justice q4 TRUE — CORRECTION TO PRIOR HYPOTHESIS) Lewis George voted YES on Secure DC Omnibus BOTH readings (Feb 6 + Mar 5 2024); ONLY Trayon White voted Present. Despite progressive profile she joined the Pinto-led public-safety coalition on this bill — Bonds/Henderson/Pinto cluster pattern. (foreign_policy_restraint q0 HARD-TRUE) FIRST DC Council member to publicly call for Gaza ceasefire Oct 12 2023; continued vocal advocacy through 2024-26; survey-response controversy March 2026 mayoral race — Article-I-restraint catch on otherwise across-the-board progressive D. (biblical_marriage q2 FALSE + q4 FALSE) Own statement: 'I have been a strong supporter of pro-LGBTQ+ bills, including making D.C. a sanctuary for people seeking gender-affirming health care'; scored 9.5/10 from GLAA (highest of any DC mayoral candidate); attended June 1 2026 LGBTQ event + Pride flag raising. (election_integrity q4 FALSE) Voted YES on D.C. Law 24-242 Local Resident Voting Rights Amendment Act Oct 2022 (12-1, Trayon White lone NO). (industry_capture q0 FALSE) Publicly promoted DC COVID vaccine requirements + pediatric vaccines (ages 5-11) in Ward 4 constituent dispatches; 'hope through the vaccine' framing in Petworth News op-ed. (border_immigration q1 FALSE) DSA-aligned 'sanctuary city' framing in 2026 mayoral platform. (family_child_sovereignty q0 FALSE) Progressive DSA-aligned platform; no public support for vouchers/charter expansion. NULL: christian_liberty all 4; life all (no direct dissent on DC reproductive-rights regime + no PP-PAC funding cite); family q1/q2/q3 (no direct vote on minor-consent/CRT/library); election q0/q1; self_defense (no specific dissent on DC firearms code); refuse_state_overreach all 5 (DC has no state); border q3/q4; industry_capture q1/q2/q3/q4; foreign_policy_restraint q1/q2/q3/q4.

2.note: Own statement: 'I have been a strong supporter of pro-LGBTQ+ bills, including making D.C. a sanctuary for people seeking gender-affirming health care.' Scored 9.5/10 from GLAA (highest of any DC mayoral candidate).

4.note: Attended June 1 2026 LGBTQ meet & greet; attended Pride flag raising ceremony; Stonewall mayoral endorsement.

4.note: Voted YES on D.C. Law 24-242 Local Resident Voting Rights Amendment Act Oct 2022 (12-1, Trayon White lone NO).

profile.religion: Jewish (publicly identified; JUFJ Campaign Fund canvassed for him; active in Washington Interfaith Network per Wikipedia)
profile.background: DC Council Ward 3 since Jan 2 2023 (succeeded Mary Cheh, 16-year incumbent). Attorney 30+ years (international trade/antitrust at Cassidy Levy Kent); Clinton State Dept appointee (democracy, human rights, women's rights, rule of law); UMich BA + GW Law JD. Long-time Ward 3 education advocate: founded Coalition for DC Public Schools and Communities (C4DC) and Ward 3 Education Network; Jackson-Reed HS planning; former ANC commissioner. Won 2022 D primary with 42.28% (8,012 votes) + Nov 2022 general 76%. Filed 2026 reelection (unopposed in June 16 2026 primary per Ward 3 Vote guide). Committees: Executive Admin & Labor; Hospital & Health Equity; Transportation & Environment; Facilities & Family Services; Housing.

matthew-frumin.notes_append: [2026-06-05 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified active incumbent (dccouncil.gov/council/ward-3-councilmember-matthew-frumin). JEWISH WARD-3 EDUCATION-ADVOCATE D — split public-safety pattern: (public_justice q1 FALSE) VOTED YES Jan 17 2023 to override Bowser veto of RCCA (12-1, Pinto lone NO); quoted: 'The RCCA modernizes our criminal code, making it more transparent and equitable, and will promote public safety' — anti-rubric direction (RCCA reduced violent-crime max penalties). (public_justice q4 TRUE) Voted YES on final Secure DC Omnibus Mar 5 2024 (12 YES, Trayon White Present); voted YES on DNA-collection-without-conviction provision; thanked Pinto for leadership — pro-public-safety direction. The 13-day-after-swearing-in RCCA override vs the year-later Secure DC YES reflects the political correction the Council rode. (family_child_sovereignty q0 FALSE) Long-time DCPS-favoring advocate; argued DCPS structurally underfunded vs charters by $40-50M/yr; founded C4DC pro-DCPS coalition; skeptical of charter funding parity — anti-school-choice direction. (biblical_marriage q2/q4 FALSE) Advocated additional FY budget funds for transgender workforce programs + youth-homelessness continuum aligned with LGBTQ services. (christian_liberty q0 HONESTLY NULL) Frumin is publicly Jewish and JUFJ-affiliated — he does not profess Christ; accurate per the rubric's honest-confidence rule, not a negative judgment. NULL: christian_liberty q1/q2/q3/q4; life all (D.C. Law 24-254 enacted Dec 2022 BEFORE Frumin's Jan 2 2023 swearing-in; no post-2023 expansion vote/quote located); marriage q0/q3; family q1/q2/q3; economic q0/q1/q3/q4 (no clean tax-hike YES/NO roll-call isolated; outspoken on Ward 3 property-tax cliff but no specific vote located); election all (D.C. Law 24-242 noncitizen voting enacted Oct 2022 before Frumin's swearing-in; no subsequent repeal vote); border all (Sanctuary Values predates Frumin 2020; no subsequent vote/statement located); self_defense all (no specific Frumin firearms vote located); industry_capture all (B24-235 minor-consent enacted Sept 2022 before Frumin; no public position on Nov 2023 DCPS COVID-vax-mandate repeal located); refuse_state_overreach all 5 (DC has no state); foreign_policy_restraint all (no DC Council Israel-Gaza cease-fire resolution vote located; JUFJ-aligned and Jewish but no specific cite); public_justice q0/q2/q3 (no clean MPD-backing quote clears HIGH BAR).

2.note: Advocated additional FY budget funds for transgender workforce programs + youth-homelessness continuum aligned with LGBTQ services.

4.note: LGBT Budget Coalition advocacy alignment; pro-LGBTQ programming posture.

0.note: Long-time DCPS-favoring advocate; argued DCPS structurally underfunded vs charters by $40-50M/yr; founded C4DC pro-DCPS coalition; skeptical of charter funding parity.

1.note: Voted YES Jan 17 2023 to override Bowser veto of RCCA (12-1, Pinto lone NO); quoted: 'The RCCA modernizes our criminal code, making it more transparent and equitable, and will promote public safety.'

4.note: Voted YES on final Secure DC Omnibus Mar 5 2024 (12 YES, Trayon White Present); voted YES on DNA-collection-without-conviction provision; thanked Pinto for leadership.
